## Shared framing: how to read the models {background-image="../../shared/assets/images/whale-shark-close.jpg" background-size="cover" background-position="center" .section-photo .shared-framing-hero}

<div class="shared-framing-panel">
<h2>Shared framing</h2>
<h3>How to read the models</h3>
<p>Before using maps or species distribution model outputs, we need a shared way to interpret them</p>
<ul>
<li>Projections are “what-if” futures</li>
<li>Uncertainty comes from the full workflow</li>
<li>Species distribution models estimate habitat suitability, not certainty</li>
<li>Outputs are decision-support tools, not final answers</li>
</ul>
</div>

---

## Projection not prediction {background-color="white" .technical-white}

::: {.sb5-header}
IKI - Save the Blue 5 (SB5)
:::

### Projection ≠ prediction {.slide-title}

::: {.projection-layout}
::: {.definition-card}
**Prediction**

“This is what will happen”
:::

::: {.definition-card}
**Projection**

“This is what could happen under these assumptions”

::: {.projection-figure}
![](../../shared/assets/images/tree-2023-projection-scenarios.png)

Conceptual schematic adapted from Schoeman et al. (2023), *Trends in Ecology & Evolution* doi:[10.1016/j.tree.2023.04.005](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.tree.2023.04.005)
:::
:::
:::

::: {.key-message}
For this workshop, climate projections help us explore plausible futures, not identify one exact future
:::

---

## Where uncertainty enters {background-color="white" .technical-white}

::: {.sb5-header}
IKI - Save the Blue 5 (SB5)
:::

### Where uncertainty enters {.slide-title}

::: {.framing-lead}
Uncertainty comes from the full chain
:::

<div class="uncertainty-figure-wrap">
<img class="uncertainty-figure" src="../../shared/assets/images/uncertainty-workflow.svg" alt="Three sources of uncertainty across the modeling workflow">
</div>

::: {.key-message}
Uncertainty is not failure; it tells us where to be cautious
:::

---

## Climate-side uncertainty {background-color="white" .technical-white .climate-choices}

::: {.sb5-header}
IKI - Save the Blue 5 (SB5)
:::

### Climate-side uncertainty {.slide-title}

<ul class="example-list">
<li><span class="main-line">Scenario: which future climate pathway?</span><span class="eg-line">e.g., SSP1-2.6, SSP2-4.5, SSP5-8.5</span></li>
<li><span class="main-line">Climate model: which global model produced the projection?</span><span class="eg-line">e.g., Earth System Models or Global Climate Models from modeling centers such as GFDL, IPSL, CESM, and others</span></li>
<li><span class="main-line">Run or ensemble member: which realization of the model?</span><span class="eg-line">e.g., r1i1p1f1, r2i1p1f1, or other ensemble members depending on the model archive</span></li>
<li><span class="main-line">Downscaling approach: how do we translate coarse projections to the regional scale?</span><span class="eg-line">e.g., delta method, statistical downscaling, and dynamical downscaling</span></li>
<li><span class="main-line">Depth and variable layer: which part of the ocean and which variable?</span><span class="eg-line">e.g., surface, mid-water, and seafloor layers for temperature, oxygen, salinity, currents, and related predictors</span></li>
</ul>

---

## Biological and species distribution model uncertainty {background-color="white" .technical-white}

::: {.sb5-header}
IKI - Save the Blue 5 (SB5)
:::

### Biological and species distribution model uncertainty {.slide-title}

- Records: where do we have data, and where are gaps?
- Sampling bias: absent, or just not sampled?
- Predictors: which variables define the niche?
- Algorithm: which statistical approach?
- Suitability: suitable does not always mean present, abundant, or critical habitat

---

## What species distribution models show {background-color="white" .technical-white}

::: {.sb5-header}
IKI - Save the Blue 5 (SB5)
:::

### Species distribution models estimate environmental suitability {.slide-title}

They do not directly show:

::: {.not-list}
- confirmed presence
- abundance
- reproduction
- population trends
- management priority by themselves
:::

::: {.key-message}
Suitable ≠ occupied ≠ important habitat
:::

---

## How we use the outputs {background-color="white" .technical-white}

::: {.sb5-header}
IKI - Save the Blue 5 (SB5)
:::

### Use model outputs as decision-support {.slide-title}

They can help identify:

- possible climate exposure
- future suitable areas
- data gaps
- areas needing monitoring
- places where planning may need to be adaptive

::: {.key-message}
Interpret outputs with expert knowledge, local context, and governance feasibility
:::

---

## Workshop rule {background-color="white" .technical-white}

::: {.sb5-header}
IKI - Save the Blue 5 (SB5)
:::

### The goal is not to find “the perfect map” {.slide-title}

The goal is to ask:

- What patterns are consistent?
- What looks uncertain?
- What needs expert review?
- What can improve in the next version?
